habitat: Evergreen and semi-evergreen forests, hill streams, shaded valleys.

description.adult_male: Glossy dark blue-black thrush with bright electric-blue shoulder patches. Stout bill.
description.adult_female: Glossy dark blue-black thrush with bright electric-blue shoulder patches. Stout bill.

behaviour: Often perches near forest streams; known for human-like whistling calls at dawn.
food: Insects, snails, small vertebrates; forages along streams.

habitat: Dense undergrowth in moist forests and plantations.

description.adult_male: Glossy black with chestnut belly and long tail; white rump highly visible.
description.adult_female: Browner above, paler below; shorter tail.

behaviour: Secretive but sings rich melodious phrases from low foliage.

habitat: Gardens, plantations, forest edges.

description.adult_male: Brown with pointed black crest, red cheek patch, and red vent. White underparts.
description.adult_female: Brown with pointed black crest, red cheek patch, and red vent. White underparts.

behaviour: Highly vocal; often perches on wires and open branches.

habitat: Dense wet evergreen forests, forest edges.

description.adult_male: Glossy black bird with a prominent crest and long tail streamers ending in distinctive 'rackets'.
description.adult_female: Glossy black bird with a prominent crest and long tail streamers ending in distinctive 'rackets'.

behaviour: A skillful mimic; often seen foraging in the canopy, sometimes with mixed-species flocks.

habitat: Open scrub, cultivation, forest edges, grasslands; favors dry, open country with scattered bushes.

description.adult_male: Ashy-grey head, rufous back and rump, narrow black mask through eye, prominent white wing patches and long, black tail. Regional subspecies can have black head or more rufous on mantle.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, may have brownish rather than pure grey head, reduced mask.
description.juvenile: Paler, buff-brown plumage, barred upperparts, faint mask, shorter tail and yellowish gape.
description.in_flight: Shows striking white wing patch and long tail. Perches, glides with rapid wingbeats, often flies low across open ground.

behaviour: Conspicuously perches on wires, poles, or bush tops. Highly territorial and aggressive. Known for impaling prey on thorns ('butchering'). Breeding season involves noisy squabbles. Calls include clear whistles, harsh 'shak-shak', chattering.
food: Large insects, lizards, small rodents, occasionally small birds.

habitat: Tall grass, marshes, wetlands, cultivation fringes; mostly in open seasonally wet areas.

description.adult_male: Tiny brown bird. In breeding plumage: bright orange-golden cap, short tail, cream below, brown streaked above. In non-breeding: longer tail, dull brown streaked crown.
description.adult_female: Similar to non-breeding male; brown cap, longer tail, paler below.
description.juvenile: Buff streaked underparts, brownish above, lacks golden crown.
description.in_flight: Small, round bird often seen briefly above grasses. Breeding males show short tails and hover or sing in flight.

behaviour: Secretive, emerges to sing from grass tops. Highly vocal, sings 'teewip' or buzzing calls during display. Males perform display flights, spiraling and singing. Nests are rounded and sewn with spider silk.
food: Small insects, spiders, larvae from grass stems and leaves.

habitat: Open scrub, gardens, secondary forest, dry deciduous woodland. Adapts to parks and villages.

description.adult_male: Dull brownish-grey, stout yellow bill, pale throat, slight streaking, pale iris. Slightly paler underparts.
description.adult_female: Indistinguishable from male.
description.juvenile: Fluffier, paler, less streaking, bright yellowish gape, food-begging calls.
description.in_flight: Slow, noisy flight; wings and tail fanned, flock keeps close and noisy.

behaviour: Highly social, forage in groups of 6-10 ('Seven Sisters'), cooperative breeding; allogrooming and sentinel behavior. Forage mainly on ground; spend most time feeding, some time on alert watching for danger. Constant chattering, alarm and food-begging calls by juveniles; dominant individuals defend territory. Roost communally.
food: Insects, seeds, berries, small fruits; occasionally takes human scraps.

photos.caption: A jungle babbler at Mangaon, Raigad
Not strictly from the Western Ghats: this is in Kawal Wildlife Sanctuary, Andhra Pradesh, India.
A Pair of Jungle Babblers pecking each other after a monsoon rain in Chalakudy, Kerala.
photos.license: Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International l

habitat: Gardens, groves, open woods, flowering trees.

description.adult_male: Glossy metallic green head/back, prominent yellow pectoral tufts, purplish-crimson rump.
description.adult_female: Dull yellowish below, olive above.

behaviour: Very active, probes flowers for nectar, capable of hovering briefly.

habitat: Forest clearing, open cultivation, towns.

description.adult_male: Dull greyish-brown plumage, tuft of feathers at the base of the bill, yellow bill/legs.
description.adult_female: Dull greyish-brown plumage, tuft of feathers at the base of the bill, yellow bill/legs.

behaviour: Highly social, forms large communal roosts, walks rather than hops on the ground.

habitat: Open country, often near cliffs, bridges, or caves for nesting.

description.adult_male: Deep blue upperparts, rufous collar and rump, heavily streaked underparts.
description.adult_female: Deep blue upperparts, rufous collar and rump, heavily streaked underparts.

behaviour: Fast aerial flyer, catches insects in flight. Builds enclosed mud nests.

habitat: Forest floor, wet undergrowth, coffee plantations.

description.adult_male: Olive-brown above, white below with two black breast bands. Unique lateral tail-wagging motion.
description.adult_female: Olive-brown above, white below with two black breast bands. Unique lateral tail-wagging motion.

behaviour: Usually solitary winter visitor; walks on the ground and constantly wags tail side-to-side.

habitat: Dry scrub, open grasslands, rocky hillsides.

description.adult_male: Streaky brown plumage, short tail, rufous wing patches visible in flight.
description.adult_female: Streaky brown plumage, short tail, rufous wing patches visible in flight.

behaviour: Terrestrial bird, known for its aerial song display flight high over the breeding territory.

habitat: Deciduous forest and woodland.

description.adult_male: Black head and upper breast, gray back, white belly.
description.adult_female: Ashy grey where the male is black.

behaviour: Forages in the canopy, often alone or in pairs. Has a slow, undulating flight.

habitat: Open broadleaf forests, gardens, groves.

description.adult_male: Vibrant golden-yellow plumage, black eye-stripe and wing markings.
description.adult_female: Duller, greener yellow with slight streaking.

behaviour: Shy and arboreal, often hard to spot despite its bright colour. Known for its clear, fluting song.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests.

description.adult_male: Brilliant grass-green body, yellow throat, black patch around the throat and beak.
description.adult_female: Brilliant grass-green body, yellow throat, black patch around the throat and beak.

behaviour: Stays high in the tree canopy; excellent mimics of other bird calls.

habitat: Shrubland, open woodland, gardens.

description.adult_male: Bright yellow underparts, black wings with two white bars. Breeding males have a black cap and back.
description.adult_female: Greener back, retains yellow/white below.

behaviour: Often found in pairs; known for their distinctive, sweet whistling call and acrobatic courtship display.

habitat: Open country, grasslands, near water and cultivation.

description.adult_male: Brilliant yellow crown and breast in breeding plumage, dark brown upperparts. (Non-breeding males resemble females).
description.adult_female: Dull sparrow-like brown.

behaviour: Colonial nesting bird; males weave intricate, pendulous nests often found in large numbers on thorny trees.

habitat: Grasslands, scrub, gardens, fields.

description.adult_male: Dark brown above, black throat, white underparts with streaking, short conical bill.
description.adult_female: Dark brown above, black throat, white underparts with streaking, short conical bill.

behaviour: Highly gregarious, feeds in large, active flocks on grass seeds. Nests communally.

habitat: Forests, foothills, open woodland.

description.adult_male: Bold crest, heavily streaked underparts, broad wings; fierce yellow eyes.
description.adult_female: Bold crest, heavily streaked underparts, broad wings; fierce yellow eyes.

behaviour: Soars above forest; hunts birds, reptiles, small mammals.

habitat: Cliffs, coasts, cliffs near ghats, open habitats.

description.adult_male: Slate-grey above, barred underparts, bold moustachial stripe.
description.adult_female: Slate-grey above, barred underparts, bold moustachial stripe.

behaviour: Fastest bird; hunts in dramatic aerial stoops (dives).

habitat: Lakes, marshes, rivers, rice fields.

description.adult_male: Slender white egret, black bill and legs, yellow feet. Breeding adults have two long plumes on the nape.
description.adult_female: Slender white egret, black bill and legs, yellow feet. Breeding adults have two long plumes on the nape.

behaviour: Actively hunts fish by dashing and prodding, often shuffles feet to disturb prey.

habitat: Marshes, ponds, wet vegetation, ditches.

description.adult_male: Grey body, pure white face and breast, red vent. Greenish legs.
description.adult_female: Grey body, pure white face and breast, red vent. Greenish legs.

behaviour: Bold, often walks on floating vegetation or near thick cover, flicks its tail constantly.

habitat: Marshes, flooded fields, large shallow lakes.

description.adult_male: White to greyish-white plumage; unique gap between the two mandibles of the bill.
description.adult_female: White to greyish-white plumage; unique gap between the two mandibles of the bill.

behaviour: Wades in shallow water, often feeding in large groups. Bill gap is used to hold and crush snails.

habitat: Shallow water, tanks, paddy fields, irrigation canals.

description.adult_male: Rufous-brown plumage, long neck, dark cap. Distinctive descending whistle call.
description.adult_female: Rufous-brown plumage, long neck, dark cap. Distinctive descending whistle call.

behaviour: Nocturnal feeder, diurnal resters, often perches on trees near water.
food: Aquatic plants, rice seeds, small invertebrates.

habitat: Large inland waters, reservoirs, rocky coastlines.

description.adult_male: Glossy black plumage, slender bill with a hook. Pale patch on the side of the head in breeding season.
description.adult_female: Glossy black plumage, slender bill with a hook. Pale patch on the side of the head in breeding season.

behaviour: Swims low in the water, dives frequently to hunt fish. Often perches with wings outstretched to dry.

habitat: Small ponds, ditches, marshes with abundant vegetation.

description.adult_male: Small, dumpy water bird; dark brown with rufous neck in breeding plumage. Lacks a visible tail.
description.adult_female: Small, dumpy water bird; dark brown with rufous neck in breeding plumage. Lacks a visible tail.

behaviour: Spends most of its time in water, dives quickly to avoid disturbance. Eats its own feathers to line the stomach.

habitat: Riverbanks, rocky streams, reservoirs.

description.adult_male: Brownish-grey above, white below; dark smudge extending up the sides of the breast. Distinctive bobbing motion.
description.adult_female: Brownish-grey above, white below; dark smudge extending up the sides of the breast. Distinctive bobbing motion.

behaviour: Solitary wader; constantly bobs its body up and down while foraging along the water's edge.

habitat: Open fields, cultivation, near water bodies.

description.adult_male: Black head and chest, white belly, prominent red fleshy wattle in front of the eye.
description.adult_female: Black head and chest, white belly, prominent red fleshy wattle in front of the eye.

behaviour: Noisy and conspicuous ground bird; known for its alarm call 'Did-he-do-it!'

habitat: Scrub, forest edges, fields, often near human habitation.

description.adult_male: Glossy black head/body, contrasting bright chestnut wings, and a very long tail. Red eyes.
description.adult_female: Glossy black head/body, contrasting bright chestnut wings, and a very long tail. Red eyes.

behaviour: Ground-feeding cuckoo; known for its deep, booming 'hoop-hoop-hoop' call. Poor flier.
food: Large insects, caterpillars, small reptiles, bird eggs.

habitat: Wetlands, fields, gardens, forest edges—often far from water.

description.adult_male: Bright blue wings/back, chocolate brown head/body, white throat and breast, large red bill.
description.adult_female: Bright blue wings/back, chocolate brown head/body, white throat and breast, large red bill.

behaviour: Common perch-hunter; dives for prey or sallies for insects from a prominent perch.
food: Fish, insects, frogs, small snakes and lizards.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests of the Western Ghats.

description.adult_male: Grey body, whitish streaked head, prominent casque above the large bill. Males have a larger, yellowish bill.
description.adult_female: Grey body, whitish streaked head, prominent casque above the large bill. Females have a slightly smaller bill.

behaviour: Arboreal and social; primarily feeds on fruit and nests in tree cavities, which the female seals. The species was uplisted to Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List in 2018 due to habitat loss, fragmentation, and population decline.

habitat: Evergreen and moist deciduous forests, wooded areas.

description.adult_male: Blue-grey head, green body, blue-green wings with a striking blue color on the rump/tail. Black neck ring and red bill.
description.adult_female: Lacks the black neck ring; bill is black.

behaviour: Flies in small flocks, often at canopy level; noisy and conspicuous.. Is endemic to the broader Indian subcontinent and not endemic to just the Western Ghats: unlike its cousin, Malabar Parakeet (Blue-winged Parakeet, Psittacula columboides).

habitat: Forest edges, cultivated fields, semi-arid habitats.

description.adult_male: Brilliant iridescent blue breast and neck; long, ornate train of tail coverts used in display.
description.adult_female: Mottled brown/white, lacks the long train.

behaviour: Omnivorous and ground-feeding; males display the train during the monsoon breeding season.

habitat: Dense evergreen and moist deciduous forests, steep ravines.

description.adult_male: Large, dark brown owl with prominent ear-tufts, heavily spotted underparts, and large yellow eyes.
description.adult_female: Large, dark brown owl with prominent ear-tufts, heavily spotted underparts, and large yellow eyes.

behaviour: Strictly nocturnal, roosting quietly during the day in thick foliage; powerful predator.
food: Large rodents, pheasants, junglefowl, and other medium-sized animals.

habitat: Towns, cities, cliffs and caves.

description.adult_male: Small, dark, sooty-brown swift with a distinct white patch on the throat and rump. Crescent-shaped in flight.
description.adult_female: Small, dark, sooty-brown swift with a distinct white patch on the throat and rump. Crescent-shaped in flight.

behaviour: Highly aerial, rarely perches, nests colonially under eaves or on high buildings/cliffs.

habitat: Open areas, near water, farmlands, riverbeds.

description.adult_male: Bright green body, reddish throat, prominent black line through the eye, long central tail feathers.
description.adult_female: Bright green body, reddish throat, prominent black line through the eye, long central tail feathers.

behaviour: Perches high, often wires, darts out to catch insects in flight. Forms large communal roosts.

habitat: Open fields, cultivation, forest clearings.

description.adult_male: Brilliant azure-blue and purple wings visible in flight; rusty brown breast and blue head.
description.adult_female: Brilliant azure-blue and purple wings visible in flight; rusty brown breast and blue head.

behaviour: Perches on prominent points (wires, dead trees) and dives to catch prey. Famous for its clumsy 'rolling' display flight.

habitat: Deciduous and evergreen forests, bamboo clumps.

description.adult_male: Unmistakable rufous-brown plumage with black barring on the flanks and wings. Male has a small red cheek patch.
description.adult_female: Unmistakable rufous-brown plumage with black barring on the flanks and wings.

behaviour: Forages mainly on ant-ridden trees; unique in using arboreal ant nests to build its own nest chamber.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests, fruiting trees.

description.adult_male: Overall green body, brown crown and cheek, distinct white streaking on the throat and cheeks.
description.adult_female: Overall green body, brown crown and cheek, distinct white streaking on the throat and cheeks.

behaviour: Arboreal, often heard but rarely seen; known for its monotonous, loud `ku-trook, ku-trook` call.

habitat: Grasslands, scrub, cultivation (often hides in dense cover).

description.adult_male: Small, quail-like bird; yellowish legs, mottled rufous and black upperparts. Females are brighter and larger.
description.adult_female: Small, quail-like bird; yellowish legs, mottled rufous and black upperparts. Females are brighter and larger.

behaviour: Terrestrial, runs quickly to escape; the female is polyandrous and uses a low, soft booming call to attract mates.

habitat: Montane evergreen forests (shola) and adjacent moist hill forests above 600m.

description.adult_male: Slaty blue body, brighter blue on the forehead and rump. Distinctive white patch at the base of the tail visible in flight.
description.adult_female: Duller blue-grey overall, less distinct white tail patch.
description.juvenile: Brownish with buff spotting; tail patch hinted.

behaviour: Perches upright and sallies out to catch insects; generally silent outside the breeding season.

habitat: Dense undergrowth and stream banks in high-elevation shola forests.

description.adult_male: Unmistakable glossy black head and upperparts contrasting sharply with brilliant orange underparts and rump.
description.adult_female: Similar to male but duller orange, and the black areas are brownish-black.

behaviour: Skulks in dense low vegetation, flicking its tail; mainly insectivorous.

habitat: Moist evergreen and semi-evergreen forests, especially fruiting fig trees.

description.adult_male: Predominantly green body; distinctive red forehead and black/white streaks on the throat. Orange eye-ring.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Arboreal; known for its ringing, territorial 'kutruk-kutruk' call. Feeds mostly on fruit.

habitat: Forests, groves, and cultivated areas with berry-producing trees.

description.adult_male: Tiny, greyish-brown above, buffy-white below. Very short, stout bill. Plain plumage contrasts with other colourful flowerpeckers.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Highly active; feeds primarily on berries and figs, playing a crucial role in seed dispersal.

habitat: Shola forests (forest patches in valleys of high-altitude grasslands); dense canopy and undergrowth above 1200m in Nilgiris, Bababudan Hills, Brahmagiris.

description.adult_male: Small, chunky bird with long legs and short tail. Dark slaty-blue upperparts, throat and breast. Warm rufous lower plumage, transitioning to buffy white at center of belly. Black lores, diffuse bluish brow.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, possibly with different iris color.
description.in_flight: Short tail and chunky body conspicuous. Low, direct flight between dense vegetation.

behaviour: Skulking species found on forest floor and dark lower canopy. Can be confiding when observed. Calls frequently with tit-like notes and harsh rattles. Song consists of shrill whistles interspersed with twangy buzzing sounds.
food: Insects and invertebrates from forest floor and low vegetation.

habitat: Dense undergrowth and edges of high-altitude shola forests (South Western Ghats).

description.adult_male: Slaty-blue upperparts, black face, clean white belly and undertail coverts. Thin dark legs.
description.adult_female: Duller blue and slightly browner on the flanks.

behaviour: Very secretive, foraging low on the ground and in dense vegetation; often only heard, singing a melodious territorial song.

habitat: Evergreen and moist deciduous forests, especially areas with fruiting trees.

description.adult_male: Olive-green overall with a lilac/pink patch on the mantle; grey cap and yellowish-green underparts.
description.adult_female: Duller, lacks the lilac patch on the mantle.
description.in_flight: Fast, direct flight; yellow feet visible.

behaviour: Arboreal and social, feeds in flocks high in the canopy on fruit.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests with tall, old trees.

description.adult_male: Very large, glossy black woodpecker with a clean white belly and prominent red crest/mustache stripe.
description.adult_female: Similar, but red restricted to the hind-crown (no red mustache).

behaviour: Drums loudly and powerfully on tall trees; solitary or in pairs, climbing trunks and large branches.

habitat: Dense, dark understorey of evergreen and moist deciduous forests.

description.adult_male: Black head, broad white breast band, brilliant scarlet-red belly/undertail. Golden-brown back.
description.adult_female: Chestnut head and breast, duller yellow-brown back, yellow belly.

behaviour: Perches upright and motionless on horizontal branches; sallies out for insects, often in the twilight hours.

habitat: Dense undergrowth, forest floor of evergreen and deciduous forests.

description.adult_male: Brilliant and multicoloured: green wings, blue rump, black head/eye stripe, buff crown, bright red vent.
description.adult_female: Slightly duller but similar.
description.in_flight: Shows flashing white patch in the wing.

behaviour: Shy and solitary, forages on the forest floor; calls loudly and clearly: 'wett-t-wett-t-tee' during the breeding season.

habitat: Dense reedbeds, scrub, and riparian vegetation along forest streams.

description.adult_male: Distinctive uniform grey head, olive-yellow body, and pale yellow undertail coverts.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Skulks in dense cover; known for its characteristic high-pitched song and chattering calls.

habitat: Forest undergrowth, dense bushes, wooded gardens.

description.adult_male: Dark grey/black body, prominent white throat patch, and a distinct white supercilium. Fanned tail with white edges.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Hyperactive; constantly fans and closes its tail in a semi-circle while hopping through foliage, catching insects.

habitat: Dry deciduous forests, open groves, roadside trees, and urban areas.

description.adult_male: Dull greyish-brown body, long tail, and a large blackish bill with a small casque/horn that is mainly black.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, with a smaller, duller casque.

behaviour: Arboreal; feeds on fruit and insects; like the Malabar Grey Hornbill, it nests in sealed tree cavities.

habitat: Dry scrub, stony hillsides, and dense forest undergrowth.

description.adult_male: Rufous-brown body with fine grey barring and white spots; grey head, red legs with multiple spurs.
description.adult_female: Duller, less spotted, lacks the prominent spurs.
description.in_flight: Short, rapid burst of flight before dropping into cover.

behaviour: Terrestrial; runs to escape rather than flies; often found in pairs; loud, cackling calls at dawn and dusk.

habitat: Moist evergreen and deciduous forests; often seen near fruiting trees.

description.adult_male: Glossy black hood, grey back, white belly, and distinctive white patch in the wing, visible in flight.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, but duller grey where the male is black.
description.in_flight: Strong, direct flight in small flocks.

behaviour: Highly social, forms flocks; feeds largely on fruit in the canopy.

habitat: Dense scrub and undergrowth in forests, especially along hillsides.

description.adult_male: Unstreaked rufous-brown cap and body, pale throat, and a relatively long tail.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Highly social, moves in small, noisy groups, foraging through thick bushes and low branches.

photos.caption: An endemic species...
photos.license: Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International l

habitat: Still or slow-moving clean water bodies: streams, lakes, ponds, backwaters.

description.adult_male: Small, electric blue upperparts, turquoise back, rufous underparts, long black bill.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, but the lower mandible (jaw) is red/orange with a black tip.
description.in_flight: Very fast, low flight over water, showing brilliant blue flash.

behaviour: Perches low and dives headfirst into the water to catch fish; solitary and highly territorial.

habitat: Forest clearings, cultivated areas near water, open woodland.

description.adult_male: Brilliant green body, prominent chestnut head and breast, black throat band, no long tail streamers.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Duller green, chestnut head less pronounced.

behaviour: Feeds high up, catching flying insects from a prominent perch; highly social.
food: Bees, wasps, and other flying insects.

habitat: Forests and groves, specializing on the trunks and branches of trees.

description.adult_male: Sleek blue-grey upperparts, purple throat/breast, bold red bill, and a black forehead patch (velvet).
description.adult_female: Similar to male, but lacks the black facial stripe.

behaviour: Unique among birds for climbing head-first down tree trunks; often part of mixed-species foraging flocks.
food: Insects and spiders gleaned from bark crevices.

habitat: Open forest, scrub, cultivated land, gardens.

description.adult_male: Black head/breast, rufous-brown body, long grey tail with black tip, grey wings with black primaries.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Undulating, clumsy flight; very long tail conspicuous.

behaviour: Loud, unmistakable 'bob-o-link' call; omnivorous and inquisitive; often seen climbing trees.
food: Fruits, seeds, insects, small reptiles, eggs, and nestlings.

habitat: Dry scrub, thorn forest, and sparse woodland edges.

description.adult_male: Black head and upperparts, white belly, bright orange-red rump and wing patches.
description.adult_female: Grey upperparts, yellow rump, black mask.
description.in_flight: Flies in small, active flocks, showing brilliant colour flashes.

behaviour: Feeds high in the canopy in mixed flocks, moving constantly.

habitat: Open wooded country, gardens, deciduous forests.

description.adult_male: Sleek, smoky grey drongo with a slightly forked tail; lacks the crest or 'rackets' of the Greater Racket-tailed Drongo.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Agile, acrobatic flyer.

behaviour: Perches conspicuously on wires or high branches, sallying out to catch insects; excellent mimic of other bird calls.

habitat: Dense evergreen and moist deciduous forests, especially in coastal and hill tracts.

description.adult_male: Large, heavy-bodied pigeon; brilliant metallic green back and wings, pale grey head and underparts, deep chestnut undertail coverts.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Heavy, powerful flight high above the canopy.

behaviour: Strictly arboreal, feeding in the canopy; gives a deep, resonant 'hoom' call.
food: Large fruits and berries, swallowed whole.

habitat: Dry scrub, thorn forest, and dense riparian vegetation.

description.adult_male: Long-tailed cuckoo; dark glossy green above, deep blue bare skin patch around the eye (face), prominent white tips to the tail feathers.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Heavy, gliding flight; often seen scrambling through branches.

behaviour: Forages methodically for insects in foliage; rarely flies long distances.
food: Large insects, caterpillars, and small vertebrates.

habitat: Open woodland, cultivation, human settlements, ruins, and old trees.

description.adult_male: Small, stocky owl; grey-brown with heavy white spotting on the crown and back. Pale facial disc and large yellow eyes.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Crepuscular and often diurnal; perches conspicuously; known for its familiar 'chiree-chiree-chiree' calls.
food: Insects, small mammals, and small birds.

habitat: Grasslands, farmlands, damp fields, always near livestock.

description.adult_male: Stocky white egret. Breeding plumage: orange-buff plumes on the head, breast, and back; reddish bill/legs.
description.adult_female: Similar, but less intense buff plumes in breeding season.
description.in_flight: Flies with neck retracted.

behaviour: Follows grazing cattle and buffalo to catch insects disturbed by their movement.
food: Insects (especially grasshoppers), spiders, and small vertebrates.

habitat: Shallow wetlands, salt pans, and flooded fields.

description.adult_male: Pied plumage: black back/wings, white head/body. **Extremely long, thin pink legs.**
description.adult_female: Similar, but the black plumage is often browner.
description.juvenile: Brown-grey where the adult is black.
description.in_flight: Long pink legs trail far behind the body.

behaviour: Wades in shallow water, sweeping its bill to find aquatic prey; graceful and vocal.
food: Aquatic insects, small crustaceans, and mollusks.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests, forest edges.

description.adult_male: Small, greenish-yellow warbler with a distinct double pale wing-bar, a prominent pale supercilium (eyebrow), and a faint crown stripe.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Active, restless, constantly gleaning insects from leaves and twigs in the middle and upper canopy.

habitat: Large rivers, reservoirs, coasts, and estuaries.

description.adult_male: Large raptor; dark brown upperparts, clean white head/underparts, and a distinct dark mask through the eye. Long, narrow wings.
description.adult_female: Similar, often with a slightly more streaked breast.
description.juvenile: Buff tips to the feathers, giving a scaly look.
description.in_flight: Wings held in an M-shape; hovers and plunge-dives for fish.

behaviour: Feeds almost exclusively on fish, plunging feet-first into water to catch prey.

habitat: Dense undergrowth, thickets, and forest floor.

description.adult_male: Brown upperparts, white throat, chestnut flanks, and a prominent, long, down-curved yellow bill (scimitar-like).
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Feeds on the ground or low in bushes; known for its loud, repetitive 'whoop-whoop' duet calls.
food: Insects and grubs found in leaf litter.

habitat: Evergreen and moist deciduous forests, often high in the canopy.

description.adult_male: Glossy black head and back, brilliant orange-red underparts, rump, and wing patches.
description.adult_female: Yellow replaces the orange-red of the male; grey head/back.
description.in_flight: Flies in loose flocks, showing striking flashes of black and orange/yellow.

behaviour: Feeds actively in the canopy, often in small groups, giving persistent whistling calls.

habitat: Dense evergreen forests of the lower hills of the Western Ghats.

description.adult_male: Black head, chestnut mantle, clean white belly, and grey rump; long black and white tail.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Arboreal and inquisitive; moves through the canopy alone or in small parties; loud, varied calls.
food: Insects, small reptiles, berries, and fruits.

habitat: Urban areas, cultivation, open country, near human habitation.

description.adult_male: Dark brown body, black hood, bright yellow bill, legs, and bare skin around the eye. Prominent white wing patches visible in flight.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: White wing patch is a key identifier.

behaviour: Bold, noisy, highly social; walks on the ground, often in pairs, feeding opportunistically.

habitat: Near water, large rivers, lakeshores, irrigation canals, village ponds.

description.adult_male: Large wagtail; black upperparts and breast, white belly. Distinctive white supercilium and long black-and-white tail.
description.adult_female: Black areas are sooty-grey.

behaviour: Walks briskly on the ground, constantly wagging its tail up and down; feeds close to water.

habitat: Dense undergrowth in subtropical and tropical moist forests, from plains to higher hills; forest edges and secondary growth.

description.adult_male: Dark brownish-grey upperparts, broad black mask through eye, white throat. Grey breast, rufous belly and underside. Uniformly colored tail darker than back. Heavy bill with pale yellow lower mandible.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Duller than adults, less distinct mask.
description.in_flight: Direct flight low through undergrowth, rarely flies in open.

behaviour: Extremely gregarious, moving in flocks of 6-40 birds. Skulks in undergrowth, feeding on ground by turning over leaves. Very vocal with loud shrill chattering, cackling and churring calls. Calls resemble those of Rufous Babbler in some instances.
food: Insects, fallen seeds, berries; forages mainly on ground among leaf litter.

habitat: Shola forests (isolated cloud forests at high altitudes) interspersed with montane grasslands; dense understory of moist evergreen forests above 1200m.

description.adult_male: Scaled pattern throughout—dark brown upperparts with buff spots forming scale-like appearance. Underparts buff-white with dark brown crescent-shaped markings creating scaled effect. Head and neck greyish with scaling.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Distinctive black band on white underwings visible in flight, similar to Siberian Thrush.

behaviour: Secretive and shy, keeping to dark understory and forest floor. Often seen on rainy days along forest roads. Forages on ground, turning over leaf litter. Scaled plumage provides excellent camouflage.
food: Opportunistic insectivore; feeds primarily on insects and other invertebrates from forest floor.

habitat: Dense montane evergreen forest (shola) and stunted rhododendron thickets at high elevations.

description.adult_male: Dark olive-brown thrush-like bird with a prominent black mask and white supercilium. Rich rufous patch on the belly/flanks.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Highly vocal, usually moving in noisy, close-knit groups; feeds on the ground and low bushes. This species has a very restricted distribution in high elevation areas of the Nilgiris and adjoining ranges

photos.caption: A Nilgiri Laughingthursh in Kothagiri, Tamilnadu, India

habitat: Evergreen and moist deciduous forests between 450-1600m elevation; also visits coffee, tea, rubber plantations and cultivated areas.

description.adult_male: Bluish-grey head, neck, breast and back; green lores and upper cheeks with bluish tinge; black band across chin with bright blue-green lower border forming collar. Blue wings and primaries. Long blue tail tipped yellow. Bright red upper mandible with yellow tip, brown lower mandible. Light yellow iris.
description.adult_female: Similar to male but smaller; breast and back greenish; lacks blue-green border below black collar; lores only faintly greenish; both mandibles blackish. Greyish-yellow underparts. Shorter tail.
description.juvenile: Greyish-green head; black collar faintly marked or absent; lacks green band below collar. Bill pale orange-red initially, turning black at 6 weeks; in males becomes red again after 9 months.
description.in_flight: Swift, straight flight through forest clearings. Long blue tail tipped yellow highly conspicuous. Dark blue wings contrast with grey body. Makes distinctive harsh screeching 'keek-keek-keek' calls while flying in flocks.

behaviour: Found in small groups of 4-5 birds or small flocks. Noisy and mostly noticed by harsh, raucous calls that differ from Plum-headed Parakeet. Almost always in tops of tall trees where difficult to detect. Only descends to ground in grain fields. Monogamous, pairing for life.
food: Seeds, fruits (especially figs, mangoes), small nuts, blossoms and nectar. Sometimes feeds on leaves of Loranthus longiflorus. May eat small insects during breeding season.

photos.caption: Male Malabar Parakeet near Mangaon, Raigad

habitat: Moist deciduous forests and sholas of Western Ghats; dense hill forests with fruiting trees.

description.adult_male: Large, dark pigeon. Blackish head, neck and underparts. Upperparts dark slaty-grey. Distinctive black and white checkerboard pattern on nape and sides of neck. Red orbital skin, red legs. Bill greyish with reddish base.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Duller than adults, less distinct checkerboard pattern on nape.
description.in_flight: Large size and dark appearance conspicuous. Heavy, direct flight through forest canopy.

behaviour: Mainly arboreal, foraging in canopy of dense hill forests. Usually seen singly, in pairs or small groups. Feeds on fruits in fruiting trees. Shy and wary, difficult to approach.
food: Frugivorous; feeds on variety of forest fruits and berries in canopy.

habitat: Dry deciduous scrub, thorny jungle, open woodland.

description.adult_male: Large woodpecker; buff/white underparts, golden-yellow back, and striking black throat/cheeks contrasting with a **white nape**. Male has a red crest.
description.adult_female: Lacks the red crest, which is black with white spots.

behaviour: Forages alone or in pairs, typically on stout trunks and thick branches; constantly drumming.

habitat: Large shallow lakes, flooded fields, marshes, river edges.

description.adult_male: Large white stork with a pinkish terminal band on the tertials (appearing pink in flight/resting). Bare yellow/orange head, deep pink bill with a down-curved tip.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Dull brown/grey, lacking the pink wash.
description.in_flight: Flies with neck outstretched, long legs trailing.

behaviour: Wades in shallow water, sweeping its half-open bill from side to side to locate prey by touch.
food: Fish, frogs, and large aquatic invertebrates.

habitat: Coastal mudflats, saline lakes, estuaries (often near the coast of the Sahyadris).

description.adult_male: Very large, pale pink/white bird with long, stilt-like legs, and a black-tipped bill bent sharply downwards.
description.adult_female: Similar, slightly smaller.
description.in_flight: Wings show striking black primaries and pink coverts.

behaviour: Feeds with its head upside down, filtering algae and small organisms from the water.

habitat: Open woodlands, cultivation with scattered trees, forest clearings.

description.adult_male: Sleek, smoky grey/ashy plumage, long pointed wings, and a short, stout blue bill with a black tip.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Glides frequently on wide, triangular wings; often seen in flocks.

behaviour: Highly aerial, catching insects in flight; noted for huddling together on branches in cold weather.

habitat: Scrub jungle, dry grasslands, stony hillsides in the rain-shadow areas.

description.adult_male: Stocky lark, streaky brown upperparts, rufous patch on the wings (visible in flight), and a distinct pale supercilium.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Aerial display flight is noisy and undulating.

behaviour: Terrestrial; often detected by its song and display flight over breeding territory.

habitat: Dense forest undergrowth, particularly along streams and wet areas.

description.adult_male: Cryptic plumage (grey/brown/white mottling), huge broad head, and a wide frog-like mouth. Males are generally greyer.
description.adult_female: Reddish-brown morph also common.

behaviour: Strictly nocturnal; roosts motionless on low branches during the day, relying on camouflage.

photos.caption: Female on the right. Thattekad, Kerala.

habitat: Montane wet evergreen forests (shola) and dense hill scrub.

description.adult_male: Deep blue-black plumage, blue metallic spots on the head, chest, and wing-coverts. Often confused with the Malabar Whistling Thrush but smaller and with a different shape.
description.adult_female: Similar, slightly duller.

behaviour: Highly secretive, forages low in dense undergrowth. Known for its clear, human-like whistling song, often mistaken for the Thrush.

habitat: Tall, undisturbed evergreen forests, especially in high-density pockets of the Western Ghats.

description.adult_male: Massive bird; mostly black with white belly, white patches on wings/tail, and a huge, bright yellow and black casque/horn above the bill.
description.adult_female: Similar, but the casque is smaller and the black marking is more extensive.
description.in_flight: Slow, powerful wingbeats producing a noticeable whooshing sound.

behaviour: Arboreal and social; feeds primarily on fruit (especially figs); noisy, territorial call.
food: Fruits (figs are favoured), small mammals, reptiles, insects.

habitat: Open plains, cultivation, low hills, and dry deciduous forests.

description.adult_male: Brown upperparts, dark streaking below, distinctive white patch on the nape/throat, and prominent **white eyes** (iris).
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Banded tail and dark patches near the wing tips visible when soaring.

behaviour: Perches conspicuously on telegraph poles or open branches, hunting insects and small vertebrates.
food: Lizards, small snakes, rodents, and large insects.

habitat: Large rivers, lakes, and estuaries with sandbanks for resting and nesting.

description.adult_male: Black crown and upperparts, white underparts, and an extremely large, **bright orange and yellow bill** with the lower mandible much longer than the upper.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Flies low over the water, skimming the surface with the lower bill to catch fish.

behaviour: Highly social; feeds in groups, flying in long, straight lines just above the water surface.

habitat: Open cultivated fields, grasslands, paddies, and short scrub.

description.adult_male: Streaky brown upperparts, buff-white breast with fine streaking, long tail, and thin legs.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Undulating flight, revealing white outer tail feathers.

behaviour: Terrestrial; walks briskly on the ground, often flicking its tail; known for its buzzing, repetitive song flight display.

habitat: Open scrub, dry stony areas, cultivated lands, often near water.

description.adult_male: Bright chestnut-red head and breast, yellow underparts, and olive-green back.
description.adult_female: Dull brownish-grey overall, yellow wash below, streaked back.

behaviour: Highly gregarious in non-breeding season, feeding on the ground in large flocks, often near grain fields.

habitat: Forest canopy, open wooded areas, gardens.

description.adult_male: Greenish-yellow warbler, distinguished by a yellowish **median crown stripe** and a distinct **double wing bar**.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.

behaviour: Highly active, constantly gleaning insects from the canopy and outer foliage.

habitat: Large shallow lakes, muddy marshes, tidal flats.

description.adult_male: Large white wading bird with long, black legs. Unmistakable long, flattened, spoon-shaped bill (yellow-tipped black).
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Flies with neck extended.

behaviour: Feeds by sweeping its bill side-to-side through shallow water to catch aquatic prey.

habitat: Open country, thin scrub, grasslands, often far from water.

description.adult_male: Bright emerald green plumage, blue tinge on the throat, black eye-line and a prominent black gorget (narrow collar). Long, slender central tail streamers.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, sometimes shorter tail streamers.
description.juvenile: Duller, lacks long tail streamers and gorget.
description.in_flight: Flies with graceful zig-zag movement; call is a gentle nasal trill.

behaviour: Perches high, sallies out to catch insects (especially bees and wasps) in mid-air; often seen dust-bathing socially.

habitat: Thick forests, well-wooded habitats, orchards, and groves.

description.adult_male: Glossy black crested head. Two morphs: Rufous (chestnut body/tail) and White (white body/tail). Both have extremely long, ribbon-like central tail streamers (up to 30 cm).
description.adult_female: Rufous back/wings, grey throat, lacks long streamers.
description.juvenile: Rufous, short tail, black throat and blue-ringed eyes.
description.in_flight: Ethereal, long tail streamers visible; chases insects in mid-air.

behaviour: Hunts insects in flight; breeds from May to July; often builds nest near drongos for protection.

habitat: Lakes, rivers, mangroves, coasts and wetlands; often around villages and towns near water.

description.adult_male: Striking raptor with rich chestnut body and wings, contrasting white head, neck and breast. Tail medium-length and slightly rounded.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, often slightly larger and bulkier.
description.juvenile: Overall dark brown above, buffy streaked below; lacks sharp white head and looks more uniformly brown.
description.in_flight: Broad wings with slightly fingered tips; white head and breast contrast with rich chestnut underwings and body.

behaviour: Often seen soaring or circling lazily over waterbodies and settlements, frequently scavenging around fish-landing sites and rubbish dumps.
food: Fish, carrion, crustaceans and other small aquatic or terrestrial animals.

photos.caption: Juvenile on a dead tree near water. Bhigwan.

habitat: Urban areas, villages, farmland, and open country; often near rubbish dumps, slaughterhouses and waterbodies.

description.adult_male: Medium-sized dark brown kite with long wings and a slightly forked tail. Pale panel in underwing, faint streaking on head and breast.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, usually a little bulkier.
description.juvenile: More heavily streaked below, paler edges to upperparts giving a slightly scaly look; tail often appears less obviously forked.
description.in_flight: Soars and glides with wings slightly raised, tail often flexed; shows long, narrow wings and shallow forked tail.

behaviour: Common scavenger; often seen circling over towns, following tractors, and snatching food in mid-air with great agility.
food: Carrion, refuse, small vertebrates, insects and offal.

habitat: Woodlands, plantations, gardens, and urban areas with tree cover.

description.adult_male: Small, compact hawk with grey upperparts, fine rufous barring on underparts and bright red eyes. Tail with dark bands and a narrow white tip.
description.adult_female: Larger, browner above with yellow to orange eyes and coarser barring below.
description.juvenile: Brown above with pale edges to feathers; underparts white with bold brown streaks rather than bars; yellow eyes.
description.in_flight: Short rounded wings and long tail; quick, powerful flapping interspersed with short glides, often weaving through trees.

behaviour: Stealthy predator of small birds, lizards and rodents; often seen perched quietly inside foliage before making rapid dashes.
food: Small birds, lizards, rodents and large insects.

habitat: Moist and dry forests, wooded hillsides, forest edges and large plantations.

description.adult_male: Dark brown eagle with small crest, yellow bare skin around eyes and on legs, and white-spotted wings. Tail with broad dark and pale bands.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, slightly larger; underparts may show more spotting or fine barring.
description.juvenile: Paler brown with more extensive white spotting and streaking, especially on head and underparts; tail banding less crisp.
description.in_flight: Broad, rounded wings and medium-length tail; distinctive broad white and dark bands in wings and tail; often soars with wings held flat.

behaviour: Typically perches quietly in forest, giving a loud, far-carrying whistling call; hunts mainly snakes and other reptiles.
food: Snakes, lizards, frogs and small mammals.

habitat: Wooded areas, forests, groves, and hillsides.

description.adult_male: Highly variable plumage, often brown/rufous. Small, pigeon-like head. Long tail with broad dark bands.
description.adult_female: Similar to male; distinguishable in close proximity.
description.in_flight: Broad, paddle-shaped wings; holds wings flat when gliding. Often confused with the Crested Honey Buzzard.

behaviour: Specialist feeder on larvae and nests of bees and wasps; feathers on head protect against stings.
food: Honeycombs, bee/wasp larvae, insects, small reptiles.

habitat: Rocky cliffs, open country, plains, and low hills (where carcasses are found).

description.adult_male: Large vulture with a pale body, dark flight feathers, and a long, slender neck. Head is mostly bald.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Very broad wings; soars high in the sky using thermals.

behaviour: Feeds communally on animal carcasses; severely threatened by poisoning from veterinary drugs (Diclofenac).

habitat: Open forest, wooded hillsides, and cultivated land.

description.adult_male: Small eagle; occurs in two morphs: pale (white belly, brown back) and dark (dark brown all over). Distinctive feathered legs ('booted').
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Similar to adult, but with softer plumage.
description.in_flight: Wings slightly pinched at the base; square tail.

behaviour: Agile hunter, often hunts from a perch or in low flight; feeds mostly on small birds and lizards.

habitat: Rocky hillsides, open forests, and dry scrub.

description.adult_male: Large eagle, grey-brown upperparts, white belly, and dark-spotted throat/chest. Large, owl-like round head and bright yellow eyes.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Long, broad wings and short tail. Often hovers motionless while hunting.

behaviour: Specializes almost entirely on snake prey, often drops to the ground to catch them.

habitat: Open country, grasslands, agricultural land, and urban edges.

description.adult_male: Small, slender falcon. Rufous-brown back with black spots, blue-grey head/tail (male), creamy streaked underparts.
description.adult_female: Browner head/tail, heavier barring than male.
description.in_flight: Pointed wings and long tail. Famous for hovering (kiting) in mid-air.

behaviour: Hunts primarily by hovering into the wind to spot small prey on the ground.

habitat: Evergreen and moist deciduous forests, sometimes moving to semi-open areas.

description.adult_male: Small, dark raptor with a prominent crest, black upperparts, and boldly barred white/black underparts (belly). White throat.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Broad, rounded wings. Distinct white patch on the throat is visible.

behaviour: Agile hunter, often feeds on insects, catching them in flight or by dropping onto foliage.

habitat: Rocky slopes, remote hills, dry open forests, and steep gorges.

description.adult_male: Large, powerful eagle. Dark brown upperparts, white underparts with sparse streaking, and a dark terminal band on the tail.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Rich rufous/chestnut underside, darkening with age.
description.in_flight: Long, square-tipped tail and narrow wings visible. Glides on flat wings.

behaviour: A fierce and fast predator, often hunting birds and small mammals in steep terrain.

habitat: Open grasslands, marshes, rice fields, and cultivation.

description.adult_male: Strikingly pied (black/white) plumage. Black head/chest, white underparts, and pale flight feathers.
description.adult_female: Brown and streaked, resembles a small buzzard, but with typical harrier shape.
description.in_flight: Flies low over the ground with wings held in a shallow 'V' shape.

behaviour: Hunts small prey while flying low and slow over open ground, often dropping down suddenly on prey.

habitat: Open steppes, agricultural fields, and dry grasslands.

description.adult_male: Smaller than Eurasian Kestrel. Male has uniform blue-grey head/tail, rufous back lacking spots.
description.adult_female: Similar to Eurasian Kestrel but generally smaller and paler.
description.in_flight: Hovers less frequently than the Eurasian Kestrel; glides more.

behaviour: Highly gregarious, often forming large roosts and feeding flocks in the winter.

habitat: Gardens, groves, scrub, thin forest, and tree-covered urban areas.

description.adult_male: Tiny, bright yellow-green bird with a prominent, distinct white ring of feathers around the eye. Yellow throat, pale greyish-white belly.
description.adult_female: Similar to male; sexes look alike.
description.in_flight: Small, rapid, flitting flight; often flies in fast, cohesive flocks.

behaviour: Highly social, found in constant motion high in the canopy, often in mixed-species foraging flocks. Gives a thin, whistling call.

habitat: The most widespread urban bulbul, found in gardens, farmlands, open woodlands, and scrub.

description.adult_male: Dark brown body with a scaly appearance, glossy black head and crest. Distinctive crimson-red patch visible under the tail (vent).
description.adult_female: Similar to male; sexes look alike.
description.juvenile: Brown head/crest; vent patch is dull orange/pink.
description.in_flight: Undulating flight pattern; the white rump is prominent.

behaviour: Bold, noisy, and highly territorial. Perches conspicuously on wires and bushes, delivering a short, sharp call.

habitat: Dense scrub, gardens, open country, and thickets.

description.adult_male: Bright olive-green back, buffy white underparts, rufous crown, and notably long central tail feathers (pin-like).
description.adult_female: Similar to male, but shorter central tail feathers.
description.in_flight: Rapid, fluttering flight low among shrubs.

behaviour: Known for stitching leaves together to form its nest pouch. Extremely vocal with a loud, repetitive 'toolip-toolip' call.
food: Small insects and spiders gleaned from leaves.

habitat: Woodlands, forest edges, and large urban parks with dense tree cover.

description.adult_male: Small, blue-grey back/wings, white underparts finely barred with reddish-brown. Yellow legs and yellow eyes.
description.adult_female: Much larger than the male; brown/grey-brown above with brown barring below.
description.in_flight: Short, broad wings and long tail; flies with a distinctive flap-flap-glide action.

behaviour: A quick, agile bird-hunter; relies on surprise, flying low and fast along hedges and edges of woods.
food: Small birds, occasionally large insects or small mammals.

habitat: Freshwater lakes, reservoirs, ponds, and slow-moving rivers.

description.adult_male: Large grey-brown duck with pale head/neck and dark cap/eye-stripe. **Black bill tipped bright yellow** with a prominent red spot at the base.
description.adult_female: Similar to male; red spot may be duller or absent.
description.juvenile: Duller plumage, less contrasting bill markings.
description.in_flight: White and dark green speculum (wing patch) visible in flight.

behaviour: Feeds primarily by dabbling (tipping tail up) in shallow water. Less gregarious than other ducks, often seen in pairs or small groups.

habitat: All types of freshwater wetlands, ponds, irrigation tanks, and reservoirs.

description.adult_male: Small, slender, glossy black cormorant with a relatively short tail and bill. Breeding adults have a slight crest and white tufts on the side of the head.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Flies low and fast, flapping with extended neck.

behaviour: Highly social, feeds and roosts in large flocks. Swims low with only neck and head visible, diving frequently to chase fish.

habitat: Coastal mudflats, estuaries, and large saline/alkaline lakes (often seen with Greater Flamingo).

description.adult_male: Smaller than Greater Flamingo. Has deep pink/crimson plumage. Bill is shorter and deeper pink.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.in_flight: Flies with neck and legs extended; smaller wingspan than the Greater Flamingo.

behaviour: Feeds primarily on specific algae (Spirulina), giving it the deeper pink color. Highly gregarious, often forming flocks of hundreds of thousands.

habitat: Paddy fields, marshes, ponds, lakes and river edges; often in human-dominated landscapes.

description.adult_male: Short-necked heron with dull brown streaked body when perched; transforms into a striking white bird in flight with bright white wings.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: More heavily streaked brown above and below; overall duller.
description.in_flight: Completely white wings and body except for brown back; slow, steady wingbeats.

behaviour: Often sits motionless near edges of water, suddenly striking at prey; very tame near villages.

habitat: Lakes, rivers, reservoirs, marshlands and coastal wetlands.

description.adult_male: Large grey heron with long neck, black supercilium and plumes, and pale grey body. Black shoulder patch and long yellowish bill.
description.adult_female: Similar to male, slightly smaller.
description.juvenile: Duller grey, lacks black head plumes, head more uniformly grey.
description.in_flight: Slow deep wingbeats, neck folded into an S-shape, long legs trailing behind.

behaviour: Stalks fish in shallow water; often stands motionless for long periods.
food: Fish, frogs, crabs, insects, small mammals.

habitat: Large rivers, reservoirs, lakes and sandbars.

description.adult_male: Graceful tern with grey upperparts, white underparts, black cap and yellow bill. Tail long and deeply forked.
description.adult_female: Identical to male.
description.juvenile: Brownish mottling above, paler cap, bill duller.
description.in_flight: Slender tern with buoyant flight; long narrow wings and deep forked tail.

behaviour: Feeds by plunge-diving in shallow waters; often rests on sandbars.

habitat: Lakes, reservoirs, marshes and slow-moving rivers with ample trees or snags.

description.adult_male: Slender waterbird with snake-like neck, blackish body, silvery streaks on wings; pointed bill.
description.adult_female: Palier neck, browner tones, less glossy.
description.juvenile: Much paler brown with faint streaks; neck dull buff.
description.in_flight: Long-necked silhouette with neck outstretched; wings broad and dark.

behaviour: Swims low in water with only neck exposed; dries wings with outstretched pose.

habitat: Dry deciduous forest, mixed scrub, orchards and open wooded areas.

description.adult_male: Bright male with grey upperparts, vivid orange underparts and rump, black wings with orange patches.
description.adult_female: Grey above, yellowish underparts, yellow wing panel.
description.juvenile: Duller versions of female with faint yellow tones.
description.in_flight: Quick, fluttery movements between canopy; flashes orange or yellow.

behaviour: Moves in small flocks; active canopy feeder.

habitat: Open scrub, grassland, agricultural edges, dry forest and village outskirts.

description.adult_male: Cryptic brown-grey nightjar with mottled plumage, white throat patch and narrow white corners on tail.
description.adult_female: Similar to male but lacks strong white tail corners.
description.juvenile: Even more cryptically patterned with softer, duller markings.
description.in_flight: Short, rounded wings; erratic fluttery flight at dusk.

behaviour: Roosts on ground in leaf litter; active at dusk and night, making distinctive ‘chuk-chuk-chuk’ calls.

habitat: Gardens, farmlands, forest edges, villages and open countryside.

description.adult_male: Grey-brown dove with pinkish underparts, white-spotted black hind-neck patch, and long tail with white corners.
description.adult_female: Similar to male.
description.juvenile: Duller, with less distinct hind-neck patch.
description.in_flight: Fast direct flight; white tail corners visible.

behaviour: Often seen in pairs or small groups; feeds on ground.
food: Seeds, grains, small fruits and insects.

habitat: Open woodland, forest edges, grassland plateaus and semi-open slopes during winter.

description.adult_male: Brown, finely streaked bird with pale buff underparts and neat breast streaking. Supercilium pale but not strong; bill base slightly pinkish.
description.adult_female: Similar to male with subtle overall dullness; same field marks.
description.in_flight: Shows white-edged tail feathers; flight is slightly undulating with soft call notes.

behaviour: Walks on the ground with deliberate steps, often perching on exposed twigs or wires. Gently bobs tail and body after landing.
food: Insects and small invertebrates; occasionally seeds.

photos.caption: Tree pipit perched on a branch

habitat: Shaded gullies, evergreen and semi-evergreen forest edges, shola patches and riparian undergrowth.

description.adult_male: Clean, crisp appearance with strong pale supercilium, olive-toned upperparts and fine neat streaking on a whitish breast.
description.adult_female: Similar to male; slight variation in tone but same diagnostic features.
description.in_flight: Rises to low branches when flushed; gives thin, high 'tsee' call.

behaviour: More arboreal than most pipits; forages quietly on forest floor and lower branches. Walks rather than hops; often flicks wings when alert.

habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests, teak plantations, wooded areas; from lowlands to 1800m elevation.

description.adult_male: Large, dark woodshrike with broad dark mask through eye, no pale supercilium. Dark grey-brown upperparts, browner wings and tail. White rump conspicuous. Underparts whitish with grey wash on breast and flanks.
description.adult_female: Similar to male. Slightly duller overall.
description.juvenile: Duller than adults with less distinct mask; brownish upperparts.
description.in_flight: White rump prominent in flight. Slow, undulating flight through canopy.

behaviour: Usually found singly or in pairs. Gleans insects from foliage in canopy and mid-storey. Sometimes joins mixed-species flocks. Calls include harsh chattering notes.
food: Insects, including caterpillars, beetles, and other arthropods gleaned from foliage.

habitat: Grassy moist highlands, mainly above 1100m; marshes, wet grasslands near sholas, reed beds.

description.adult_male: Uniform brown upperparts with buff supercilium. Broad, round-tipped and long graduated tail with thin dark bars. Underside of tail very dark, feathers tipped white. Whitish underparts with buff on breast and flanks. Black gape visible when singing.
description.adult_female: Similar to male but gape brown, not black.
description.in_flight: Broad tail fanned in display flights. Male performs fluttering song flight with fanned tail during breeding season.

behaviour: Skulking bird that moves rapidly through grass and reeds in non-breeding season. During breeding, males perform conspicuous song flights, flying up into air with fanned tail and fluttering wings while delivering lark-like trilled song. Calls include 'chack' and 'zink' notes.
food: Insects gleaned from grass stems and ground.

photos.caption: Bramhagiri Wildlife Sanctuary, Karnataka
photos.license: Released in public domain by author

habitat: Moist deciduous, semi-evergreen and riverine forests of the Western Ghats foothills and lowlands; often along rivers, fruiting fig trees, and open forest edges. Occurs up to mid-elevations but largely a low-elevation species.

description.adult_male: Glossy black body with large white belly patch and white tips to the primaries and tail. Massive pale bill with a prominent casque: the male’s casque is large, blocky, and mostly pale yellowish with extensive black on the front. Facial skin around eye bluish. Tail long and white with a bold black subterminal band. Powerful wingbeats audible in flight.
description.adult_female: Similar to male but casque smaller and less bulky, with reduced black marking on the front; overall appearance slightly smaller and bill proportionately narrower.
description.juvenile: Duller blackish-brown plumage, smaller bill with minimal casque development, whitish underparts less sharply demarcated. Flight feathers and tail patterns develop with age.
description.in_flight: Heavy, undulating flight with strong wingbeats producing a distinct whooshing sound. White primaries and tail tips flash prominently; flight silhouette shows long tail and heavy bill.

behaviour: Usually seen in pairs or small family groups; may join mixed-frugivore flocks at large fruiting trees. Strong preference for figs and other large native fruits but also takes insects, small vertebrates, and occasionally raids nests. Nesting involves the female sealing herself inside a tree cavity with a wall of mud and droppings, leaving only a narrow slit for the male to pass food; she and the chicks remain sealed until the young are nearly grown. Highly dependent on mature forest and large nest trees. Often perches conspicuously on tall emergent trees near rivers and forest edges.
food: Fruits (especially figs), berries and drupes; also insects, lizards, small mammals, and occasionally eggs or nestlings. Diet dominated by fruit for most of the year.

photos.caption: Adult perched near fruiting fig tree, Western Ghats foothills
